lesson: A first-start overcurrent with the barrel frozen solid means an interrupted overnight standby cycle, not a dead capacitor. Look inside and meter the motor before swapping parts — defrost and have a word with the closing shift.
symptoms: Trips out after a few seconds.
Opening shift found the barrel locked solid.
Motor protection trips as soon as the beater tries to move.
Machine served normally at close last night.
Machine starts with a heavy hum, then shuts itself down.
Nothing will dispense from the frozen side.
This is the first start since yesterday.

tests.error-log: BEATER OVERCURRENT trips on every start attempt. Nothing else unusual in the log.
tests.temp-probe: Barrel at −15 °C. That's not soft serve, that's a glacier.
tests.inspect-beater: Hard ice built up on the barrel wall, and the beater binds against it when you turn it by hand.

flavour: The machine wasn't broken. It was hibernating.
authenticityNote: Skip the overnight standby mode and the barrel freezes into a block; the stalled beater presents exactly like a dead run capacitor until you look inside or meter the motor. Defrost and a stern word with the closing shift.
